title: Beautiful Savior

metadata.composer: 'Crusader`s Hymn' Silesian folk song, 1842 Richard S. Willis, 1850
metadata.lyricist: German Jesuits, published 1677. Translated by Joseph A. Seiss, 1873
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2005 Revision.
c_lines: Words: German Jesuits, published 1677. Translated by Joseph A. Seiss, 1873.
Music: 'Crusader`s Hymn' Silesian folk song, 1842.  Setting: Richard S. Willis, 1850.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2005 Revision.

1.raw_text: Beautiful Savior, King of Crea- tion Son of God and Son of Man! Truly I'd love Thee, truly I'd serve Thee, Light of my soul, my joy, my crown.

2.raw_text: Fair are the meadows, Fair are the woodlands, Robed in the flow'rs of blooming spring; Jesus is fairer, Jesus is purer, He makes our sorr'wing spirit sing.

3.raw_text: Fair is the sunshine, Fair is the moonlight, Bright the sparkling stars on high; Jesus shines brighter, Jesus shines purer Than all the angels in the sky.

4.raw_text: Beautiful Savior, Lord of the nations, Son of God and Son of Man! Glory and honor, Praise, adora- tion Now and forevermore be Thine!
